entries.definition:
كتاب العين

عرف

وخلّفنا المَعَارِض والهضابا

أقول لصاحبيَّ وقد هبطنا

(عرف) العين والراء والفاء أصلان صحيحان، يدلُّ أحدُهما على تتابُع الشيء متَّصلاً بعضُه ببعض، والآخر على السكون والطُّمَأنينة.

فالأوّل العُرْف: عُرْف الفَرَس. وسمِّي بذلك لتتابُع الشَّعر عليه. ويقال: جاءَت القَطا عُرْفاً عُرْفاً، أي بعضُها خَلْفَ بعض.

ومن الباب: العُرْفة وجمعها عُرَف، وهي أرضٌ منقادة مرتفِعة بين سَهْلتين تنبت، كأنّها عُرف فَرَس. ومن الشِّعر في ذلك

والأصل الآخر المعَرِفة والعِرفان. تقول: عَرَف فلانٌ فلاناً عِرفاناً ومَعرِفة. وهذا أمر معروف. وهذا يدلُّ على ما قلناه من سُكونه إليه، لأنَّ مَن أنكر شيئاً توحَّشَ منه ونَبَا عنْه.

ومن الباب العَرْف، وهي الرَّائحة الطيِّبة. وهي القياس، لأنَّ النَّفس تسكُن إليها. يقال: ماأطيَبَ عَرْفَه. قال الله سبحانه وتعالى: ] وَيُدْخِلُهُمُ الجَنَّةَ عَرَّفَهَا لَهُمْ [ [محمد 6]، أي طيَّبَها. قال:

بواضحةِ الخدّين طيِّبة العَرْفِ

إِلا رُبَّ يومٍ قد لَهَوْتُ ولَيْلَةٍ

والعُرْف: المعروف، وسمِّي بذلك لأنَّ النفوس تسكُن إليه. قال النابغة:

فلا النُّكْرُ معروفُ ولا العُرْف ضائعُ

أبَى اللهُ إِلاَّ عدلَه ووفاءه

ephemeral riches and pleasures of this life [as opposed to that of
the Hereafter] 2 an errand, gain, benefit (9:42) لو كان عرضا قريبا
were it a gain near at hand.

عَرِيض ʿarīḍ [quasi-act. part.] extensive, far reaching (41:51) فذو
دعاء عريض he is full of prolonged prayer.

عَارِض ʿāriḍ [act. part.] approaching on a broad front, a cloud
traversing the sky (46:24) فلما رأوه عارضا مستقبل أوديتهم when they
saw it as a traversing cloud approaching their valleys.

عُرْضَة ʿurḍatun [n.] ready subject, butt; obstacle (2:224) ولا
تجعلوا الله عرضة لأيمانكم broadly interpreted either as: do not make
God the butt of your oaths or, do not make God-backed oaths [not
to perform a charitable act] an obstacle between you and doing
good.
entries.definition_english:
ephemeral riches and pleasures of this life [as opposed to that of
the Hereafter] 2 an errand, gain, benefit (9:42) لو كان عرضا قريبا
were it a gain near at hand.

عَرِيض ʿarīḍ [quasi-act. part.] extensive, far reaching (41:51) فذو
دعاء عريض he is full of prolonged prayer.

عَارِض ʿāriḍ [act. part.] approaching on a broad front, a cloud
traversing the sky (46:24) فلما رأوه عارضا مستقبل أوديتهم when they
saw it as a traversing cloud approaching their valleys.

عُرْضَة ʿurḍatun [n.] ready subject, butt; obstacle (2:224) ولا
تجعلوا الله عرضة لأيمانكم broadly interpreted either as: do not make
God the butt of your oaths or, do not make God-backed oaths [not
to perform a charitable act] an obstacle between you and doing
good.

entries.definition:
ʿ–r–f heights, facial features, mane; comb or crest of a bird; to
recognise, to know, knowledge, to inform; mentor; divinations; to
confess, confession; social norms, good deeds, charity; fragrance,
perfumes. Of this root, 10 forms occur 70 times in the Qur’an:
عَرَفَ ʿarafa 18 times; يُعْرَف yuʿraf twice; عَرَّفَ ʿarrafa twice; تَعَارَفُوا
taʿārafū twice; اعترفنا iʿtarafnā twice; عُرْف ʿurf twice; مَعْرُوف
maʿrūf 38 times; مَعْرُوفَة maʿrūfatun once; الأَعْرَاف ʾal-ʾaʿrāf twice
and عَرَفَات ʿarafāt once.

عَرَفَ ʿarafa i [v. trans.] 1 to know (2:146) يعرفونه كما يعرفون أبناءهم
they know it as well as they know their own children 2 to
recognise (12:58) ودخلوا عليه فعرفهم and they came in to him and he
recognised them 3 to know as (2:89) فلما جاءهم ما عرفوا كفروا به but
when there came to them something they knew [to be true] they
rejected it 4 to recognise, to acknowledge, take to task (in a
variant reading of 66:3) عرف بعضه وأعرض عن بعض he took her to
task for part of it (or, acknowledged [lit. recognised]) part of it
and ignored the other.

يُعْرَف yuʿraf [pass. v.] to be recognised (33:59) ذلك أدنى أن يعرفن
this is more likely to make them recognisable.

عَرَّفَ ʿarrafa [v. II trans.] 1 to make known to someone (47:6)
ويدخلهم الجنة عرفها لهم He will admit them to the Garden [which] He

614

they know it as well as they know their own children 2 to
recognise (12:58) ودخلوا عليه فعرفهم and they came in to him and he
recognised them 3 to know as (2:89) فلما جاءهم ما عرفوا كفروا به but

rejected it 4 to recognise, to acknowledge, take to task (in a

this is more likely to make them recognisable.

عَرَّفَ ʿarrafa [v. II trans.] 1 to make known to someone (47:6)
ويدخلهم الجنة عرفها لهم He will admit them to the Garden [which] He

614

entries.definition:
had made known to them 2 to take to task for, to point out an error
(66:3) عرف بعضه وأعرض عن بعض he took [her] to task for (or,
acknowledged/pointed out an error [lit. part of it]) and ignored
the other 3 to make fragrant (according to an interpretation of
verse 47:6) ويدخلهم الجنة عرفها لهم He will admit them to the Garden
[which] He had made fragrant (or, made familiar) for them.

يَتَعَارَف yataʿāraf [imperf. of v. VI, intrans. with prefix ت elided]
to become acquainted with one another, to get to know one
another, to recognise one another (49:13) وجعلناكم شعوبا وقبائل لتعارفوا
and We ordered you into nations and tribes, that you might get to
know one another.

اعْتَرَفَ iʿtarafa [v. VIII, intrans.] to confess, to acknowledge
(9:102) وآخرون اعترفوا بذنوبهم and others confessed their sins.

عُرْف ʿurf [v. n./n.] 1 norm, goodness (7:199) خذ العفو وأمر بالعرف
accept [from people] what comes easy [to them] (or, take to
forgiveness) and enjoin goodness 2 [adverbially] like the mane of
a horse, in succession, in a continuous series; in goodness
(77:1) والمرسلات عرفا by the [winds] sent forth in succession (or,
for spreading goodness).

مَعْرُوف maʿrūf I [pass. part./v. n.] 1 recognised norms,
established principles, fairness (2:228) ولهن مثل الذي عليهن بالمعروف
and due to them [the wives] are [rights] similar to that which is
incumbent upon them, according to what is recognised [to be
fair] 2 [adjectival] charitable, compassionate, kind (2:263) قول
معروف ومغفرة a kind word [from a would-be giver] and forgiveness
[from the would-be receiver of charity] II [ n., with the generic
definite article المعروف in opposition to المنكر al-munkar (q.v.)]
goodness, virtues, actions or deeds the goodness of which is
established by the accepted norms (3:104) ويأمرون بالمعروف وينهون
عن المنكر they enjoin what is right, and forbid what is wrong.

مَعْرُوفَة maʿrūfatun [fem. pass. part.] known; reasonable, clear
(24:53) قل لا تقسموا طاعة معروفة tell them, ‘Swear not; [it is]
reasonable obedience [that is required]’.

الأَعْرَاف ʾal-ʾaʿrāf [pl. of n. عُرْف ʿurf] heights, uppermost parts

615
